title: Time to Scheme — One-Line Manifesto
html:
TL;DR

  • A single-sentence Google Doc dated March 30, 2026: "Ok we might be cooked, but it's also might be time to double down."
  • Manifesto-fragment — sits in the same one-line genre as I am God's eyes, I am a fucking killer, and the longer fall backwards into the void cluster.
  • Lands ~6 days after the Emily misalignment list and ~2 weeks after the True AI Result Accuracy Rankett deep dive — sandwiched between relationship doubt and Rankett technical-uncertainty.
  • The "we might be cooked" signals Yahir naming external pressure (could be Rankett product progress, runway, Emily, or all of it); "time to double down" is the decisive frame he picks rather than the retreat-frame.
  • The single-line format is itself the message — Yahir doesn't need 2,000 words to make the call.

Core content

The doc is 16 words. There is no other content. "Ok we might be cooked" uses the "cooked" slang that runs through founder-Twitter — Yahir has internalized the X-discourse vocabulary that the CV cohort application confirmed is his primary inbound surface. "Double down" is a poker-derived bet-the-stack frame; the parallel earlier instances of this frame include the "max effort" convention and the max effort commit concept.

Pairs structurally with 2026 03 30 Untitled document siblings (if any same-week notes exist) — but stands alone as a decision-fork artifact. Yahir is on record naming the binary (retreat or double-down) and choosing the second.

Pattern observed

The one-line decisive-frame manifesto is a recurring Yahir genre. Across 2026 alone: Jan 22 killer, Feb 26 fall backwards, March 30 double-down, April 14 God's eyes. Cadence: roughly one per month. Each lands at a stress inflection. Together they form the decision-fork journal strand of the corpus.

Privacy

  • No PII; no specifics.

Connections

  • — genre parent
  • rankett — most likely subject
  • max effort commit · memento mori decision filter — decision-frame sisters
  • 2026 01 22 i am a fucking killer · 2026 02 26 fall backwards into void · 2026 04 14 i am gods eyes — sibling one-liners
  • 2026 03 13 true ai result accuracy — Rankett technical-uncertainty context
  • 2026 03 24 alignment misalignment — adjacent Emily-context
  • — taxonomy-cousin (one-liner subtype)
